title: Combine data from multiple data sources

blocks.content:
Welcome to the Grafana learning path that walks you through combining data from multiple data sources into a single panel.

Most infrastructure environments use more than one data backend. Metrics might live in Prometheus, business data in PostgreSQL, and logs in Loki. This path guides you through using the Mixed data source and Join by field transformation to bring query results from different sources together in one visualization.

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this path, you'll be able to:

- Select the Mixed data source to query multiple backends in one panel
- Add queries from different data sources and identify them by RefID
- Apply a Join by field transformation to combine query results on a shared column
- Use Table view to inspect and verify the combined output

## Before you begin

Before you combine data from multiple sources, ensure you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create one, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account).
- Basic familiarity with creating panels and running queries in Grafana.

This path uses the built-in Grafana (TestData) data source, so no external data source configuration is required.

## Troubleshooting

If you get stuck, we've got your back! Where appropriate, troubleshooting information is just a click away.

## More to explore

We understand you might want to explore other capabilities not strictly on this path. We'll provide you opportunities where it makes sense.
